name: Commodity (summary)
urlEvidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572, action 27 navigation log — navigated to "https://importnotification-static-snd.azure.defra.cloud/notification/vnet/protected/notifications/DRAFT.GB.2026.1526011/commodity/summary". ADDED BY THE ADVERSARIAL PASS: the previous revision asserted this URL with no citation at all. It is correct, and this is the citation. Note the page is reached by the submit on the preceding 'Variety and class of commodity' page (action 27), and left by the submit at action 28 — which is why action 28's own navigation log reads 'page-5' and why a careless reader could mis-attribute the snapshot.
pageTitle: Commodity - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
caption: Description of the goods
heading: Commodity
secondaryActionsNote: 'Add another Genus (and Species)' and 'Add another commodity' are confirmed rendered (trace e10b7cd5, action 28). 'Remove' is template-inferred only (commoditySummary.html:59) and was never rendered in any trace — see the remove_species_<uniqueComplementId> field.

structure.text: You have added the maximum amount of commodity lines. You cannot add another variety and class.
structure.govukComponent: Body text (class="commodity-details-lines-remaining govuk-body-l govuk-!-display-inline-block", id=commodity-details-lines-remaining). Rendered ONLY when maxCommodityLinesReached; never observed in any trace. Sits inside the form, after the hidden inputs and before the first commodity group. Copy verified VERBATIM against commoditySummary.html:27-31 by the adversarial pass — it is not invented. CORRECTION: the previous revision recorded only the id and missed that `commodity-details-lines-remaining` is ALSO a bespoke class — see nonStandardPatterns.

Heading (h2.govuk-heading-m) — group heading above the table for this commodity code. The text is {{commodity.commodityDescription}}, i.e. the description of that commodity code, NOT fixed copy (commoditySummary.html:34, re-verified). Repeats once per distinct commodity code.

structure.text: Commodity summary table | Commodity code / Genus (and Species) / EPPO code / Variety / Class / Remove action links :: 08059000 / Citrus australasica / CIDAC / None / Extra Class / (empty)
structure.govukComponent: Table (govuk-table govuk-!-margin-bottom-3 commodity-summary-table). Visually-hidden caption 'Commodity summary table'. id=commodity-summary-table-08059000 — id is suffixed with the commodity code, so one table renders per commodity code. Every string re-verified character-for-character against the action 28 snapshot.

Link (a.govuk-link.govuk-link--no-visited-state) inside a p.govuk-body; href -> /notification/vnet/protected/notifications/:draftId/consignment/page-4?commodity-selected-code=08059000. NOTE: this link does NOT carry govuk-!-display-inline-block or link-button — only 'Add another commodity' does.

Button (govuk-button govuk-!-margin-bottom-4 govuk-!-margin-right-3). type=submit, name=save-button, value='Save and continue', id=button-save-and-continue, data-module=govuk-button, data-prevent-double-click=true. CORRECTION: the previous revision omitted data-module="govuk-button" — the attribute that wires the govuk-frontend Button JS module (double-click prevention). Wrapped in div.govuk-!-margin-bottom-8.
Link styled as a button (a.govuk-link.govuk-link--no-visited-state.link-button.govuk-!-display-inline-block); href -> /notification/vnet/protected/notifications/:draftId/consignment/page-3

govukComponents.usage: Lists each Genus (and Species) row captured for a commodity code — commodity code, genus/species, EPPO code, variety, class, plus a final column holding the per-row Remove control (empty in the only observed trace).
govukComponents.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572, action 28 (before/input snapshot) — re-verified by the adversarial pass via eval of main.outerHTML.

govukComponents.usage: Primary submit — posts the form and navigates to /consignment/page-5 (Purpose of the consignment) on the observed branch. This is the ONLY save control the template renders, in every state (commoditySummary.html:81-85) — see openQuestions.
Back navigation, rendered OUTSIDE <main> as page furniture. href="#" with id=back-link — JS-driven history back, not a server-rendered target URL.
govukComponents.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572, action 28 — re-verified by eval: Back

govukComponents.usage: Section caption 'Description of the goods' above the H1.

H1 'Commodity' (govuk-heading-xl) and H2 'Other' (govuk-heading-m) grouping the table. NOTE: govuk-heading-m is a sibling size class, not a modifier of govuk-heading-xl — they are two separate headings, recorded here under one component entry.

'Add another Genus (and Species)' (adds a row to this commodity code) and 'Add another commodity' (starts a new commodity code). CORRECTION: the modifier list is a UNION across the two links, not a set both carry — 'Add another Genus (and Species)' is plain govuk-link govuk-link--no-visited-state, while ONLY 'Add another commodity' carries link-button and govuk-!-display-inline-block. The previous revision listed govuk-!-display-inline-block without that caveat and omitted link-button entirely. Count is scoped to <main>; the page furniture outside main carries further links.
govukComponents.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572, action 28 — per-link classes re-verified by eval of main.outerHTML.

govukComponents.usage: Table caption 'Commodity summary table' and the 'Remove action links' column header text.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Nothing — it is a dead class. The GOV.UK override utility is 'govuk-!-margin-bottom-9' (hyphen after the bang). This one is missing that hyphen, so no CSS matches it.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: A genuine typo bug in the IPAFFS template, sitting directly next to a correctly-spelled 'govuk-!-margin-bottom-9' on the parent div (which is why the spacing looks fine and the bug went unnoticed). Do not port it. It is a good example of why the rebuild should use govuk-frontend Nunjucks macros and utility classes rather than hand-typed class strings.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572, action 28 — main.html: <div class="govuk-!-margin-bottom-9"><div class="table-responsive govuk-!margin-bottom-9">. Both spellings re-verified in the same snapshot by the adversarial pass, and in source at commoditySummary.html:36.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Custom class that makes the 'Add another commodity' anchor render like a button, sat next to the real govuk-button. Paired with govuk-!-display-inline-block to get the two onto one line.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: An <a> dressed as a button — it navigates (GET) but looks like a submit control, which is the classic anti-pattern the Design System warns against: it breaks keyboard/AT expectations (Enter vs Space) and blurs 'this submits my data' against 'this takes me elsewhere'. The new app should either use a real secondary button, or leave it as an honest govuk-link.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-button with govuk-button--secondary (if it should look like a button), or a plain govuk-link (if it is genuinely navigation). The Design System's Button component supports rendering as an <a> via the href option, which is the supported way to get this look.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572, action 28 — main.html: Add another commodity. Source: commoditySummary.html:78.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Custom wrapper div around the govuk-table, almost certainly providing horizontal overflow scrolling on narrow viewports (a Bootstrap-style idiom).
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Not a Design System pattern. The GOV.UK Table has no built-in responsive wrapper; the Design System guidance is to reduce the number of columns or restructure rather than side-scroll. With 6 columns this table is a candidate for that rethink in the new app.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: No direct equivalent. Either keep an overflow-x container as a deliberate, documented deviation, or reduce columns. Note the rebuild brief already contemplates a simpler model.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572, action 28 — main.html: <div class="table-responsive govuk-!margin-bottom-9">. Source: commoditySummary.html:36.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Custom alignment class on the final (Remove action) table cell, right-aligning the action link.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Bespoke utility duplicating a utility the Design System already ships.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572, action 28 — main.html: <td class="govuk-table__cell text-align-right">

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Bespoke class on the max-commodity-lines notice paragraph, duplicating the element's own id as a styling/selection hook: class="commodity-details-lines-remaining govuk-body-l govuk-!-display-inline-block".
nonStandardPatterns.concern: ADDED BY THE ADVERSARIAL PASS — the previous revision recorded only id=commodity-details-lines-remaining and missed the identically-named class. Low risk in itself, but it is the same id-and-class duplication smell as commodity-summary-page below, and it sits on the one piece of copy in this spec that no trace has ever rendered.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: n/a — the govuk-body-l class already does the visual work; drop the bespoke hook or make it a data attribute.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/commoditySummary.html:28. INFERRED from template only — never rendered in any trace.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: IPAFFS-specific hooks for page- and table-scoped styling/selection. The table's id is suffixed with the commodity code (id=commodity-summary-table-08059000).
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Low risk — these are naming/selection hooks, not visual deviations. Worth noting only because the code-suffixed id encodes the one-table-per-commodity-code structure, which the new app must reproduce as data structure rather than as DOM ids.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: n/a — keep as a data-attribute or scoped id in the rebuild rather than a styling hook.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572, action 28 — main.html: <main class="govuk-main-wrapper commodity-summary-page" id="main-content">, <div class="govuk-grid-row" id="commodity-summary-page">, <table id="commodity-summary-table-08059000" class="govuk-table govuk-!-margin-bottom-3 commodity-summary-table">. All three re-verified.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: link-button on a <button type="submit"> (the row-level Remove control)
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The SAME bespoke link-button class flagged above is also applied in the OPPOSITE direction — to make a real submit button render as a link for the per-row Remove action.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: The class is used to invert affordances BOTH ways on one page: an <a> dressed as a button ('Add another commodity') and a <button> dressed as a link ('Remove'). The Remove control genuinely must stay a submit button — it POSTs a destructive state change (commodity_summary.js:24-42) — so disguising it as a link is the more harmful of the two: it advertises navigation while performing a mutation. This pairing is the clearest single anti-pattern on the page and the rebuild should not carry the shared cosmetic class across.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Summary list row actions (govuk-summary-list__actions) for Remove — the Design System's own idiom for per-item remove in an 'Add another' collection.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/commoditySummary.html:59 — <button type="submit" name="remove_species_{{variations.uniqueComplementId}}" value="Remove" class="link-button" id="remove-species-{{variations.uniqueComplementId}}">Remove</button>. Line number re-verified by the adversarial pass. INFERRED from template only — never rendered in any trace (gated on variations.length > 1 at :58).

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The table uses govuk-table, govuk-table__head, govuk-table__header and govuk-table__cell but omits govuk-table__row on every row, in both <thead> and <tbody>.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Evidence the table is hand-written HTML rather than the govukTable Nunjucks macro, so its class set has drifted from the Design System's own output. Harmless today, but it will silently miss any future govuk-frontend row styling. Using the macro in the new app makes this class of drift structurally impossible — which is the general lesson from the govuk-!margin-bottom-9 typo above too. (Scope note added by the adversarial pass: the page is not wholly macro-free — the save button does carry data-module="govuk-button" and is JS-initialised. It is the TABLE specifically that is hand-rolled.)
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: The govukTable macro, which emits govuk-table__row itself.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572, action 28 — main.html renders <tr> with no class attribute, re-verified. Corroborated at commoditySummary.html:40,51.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: Editable add/remove collection rendered as a raw <table> rather than an 'Add another' summary list
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Presents a mutable collection (commodity code -> many variety/class lines, each individually removable) as a 6-column data grid with an inline submit per row.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: The page's central design decision and the one most worth revisiting. GOV.UK's established pattern for a mutable collection is a summary list (or summary card) per item with Change/Remove actions — not a grid. Note that two of the other non-standard patterns on this page exist ONLY to prop this one up: table-responsive (because 6 columns overflow on mobile) and text-align-right (to place the Remove control). Fix the pattern and both disappear.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: The 'Add another' pattern — one govuk-summary-list per commodity line with Remove actions, plus an 'Add another' button.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Custom modifier positioning the back link alongside a breadcrumb trail; the link itself has no real target and relies on JS to go back in history.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: href="#" means the back link is inert without JavaScript and gives no meaningful target to assistive tech or to a middle-click. The Design System expects a real URL. Also, GOV.UK guidance is to use either a back link or breadcrumbs, not both together — this modifier exists precisely to reconcile that.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-back-link with a genuine server-rendered href to the previous page in the journey.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572, action 28 — Back. Re-verified by eval.

fields.name: crumb
fields.label: (no visible label — hidden input)
fields.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572, action 28 — <input name="crumb" type="hidden">. CSRF token (hapi crumb plugin). Platform mechanism, not a user requirement; value is a live credential and is deliberately not transcribed ([REDACTED]). Re-verified present by the adversarial pass's independent control inventory.

fields.name: etag
fields.label: (no visible label — hidden input)
fields.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572, action 28 — <input name="etag" type="hidden" value=""0000000000BD2B55"">. Value re-verified exactly. Optimistic-concurrency token round-tripped on every POST; the new app needs an equivalent concurrency story but not necessarily this mechanism.

fields.name: commodityDetailsPage
fields.label: (no visible label — hidden input)
fields.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572, action 28 — <input name="commodityDetailsPage" type="hidden" value="">. Empty in this trace; re-verified. Pagination/return-position marker; the same param appears as a query string on the later Consignment Details page (?commodityDetailsPage=1).

fields.name: fromImporterReview
fields.label: (no visible label — hidden input)
fields.conditionalOn: Rendered only when actionsModel.fromImporterReview is set — i.e. the user reached this page from the importer review screen. Mutually exclusive with fromPartTwoOverview (if/else if).
fields.evidence: /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/commoditySummary.html:21-22 — re-verified verbatim by the adversarial pass. Not present in the observed trace. Carries 'where I came from' so the submit can return the user there rather than continue the linear journey (formActions.uriFromOverview, commodity_summary.js:48-53) — this is the mechanism behind the change-answer-and-return behaviour the rebuild will need in some form. Corroborated by the app's own unit test, which drives request.query.fromImporterReview and asserts the returned links carry ?fromImporterReview=true (test/handlers/importer/commodity_summary_test.js:146-153).

fields.name: fromPartTwoOverview
fields.label: (no visible label — hidden input)
fields.conditionalOn: Rendered only when actionsModel.fromImporterReview is ABSENT and actionsModel.fromPartTwoOverview is set. Mutually exclusive with fromImporterReview.
fields.evidence: /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/commoditySummary.html:23-24 — re-verified verbatim. Not present in the observed trace. Same return-to-origin mechanism as fromImporterReview, for the Part Two overview screen.

fields.name: remove_species_<uniqueComplementId>
fields.label: Remove
fields.conditionalOn: Rendered per row ONLY when that commodity has more than one variation ({{#gt commodity.variations.length 1}}, commoditySummary.html:58) — i.e. you cannot remove your last remaining line. This explains the empty <td> observed in the trace: it is the single-row suppressed state.
fields.evidence: /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/commoditySummary.html:58-59 (gate at :58, button at :59 — line numbers re-verified by the adversarial pass) — <button type="submit" name="remove_species_{{variations.uniqueComplementId}}" value="Remove" class="link-button" id="remove-species-{{variations.uniqueComplementId}}">Remove</button>. Handler at commodity_summary.js:24,28-42 (line 24 re-verified: `const removeAction = Object.keys(payload).find(x => payload[x] === 'Remove')`): it finds the remove action by scanning the payload for ANY key whose value === 'Remove', then splits the key on '_' to recover uniqueComplementId, patches the notification, and redirects back to this same page. NOTE FOR THE REBUILD: the remove is applied IMMEDIATELY with no confirmation interstitial — a destructive action with no 'Are you sure?'. That is a design decision to take deliberately, not inherit. Template-inferred only; never rendered in any trace and never exercised by any Playwright/E2E test.

fields.name: save-button
fields.label: Save and continue
fields.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572, action 28 — <button type="submit" name="save-button" value="Save and continue" id="button-save-and-continue" class="govuk-button govuk-!-margin-bottom-4 govuk-!-margin-right-3" data-module="govuk-button" data-prevent-double-click="true">Save and continue</button>. Re-verified verbatim from both the rendered DOM and commoditySummary.html:82. The button posts its own name/value so the server can distinguish which submit control was used — the OTHER controls being the per-row Remove buttons, and nothing else (see openQuestions: this is now closed from source). Corroboration: no page object in the QA repo targets this button by id/name — the workflow clicks it via the generic role locator borrowed from another page object (page-objects/notification/CommodityBasicDescriptionPage.ts:34-36, driven at workflows/notification/ched-pp-workflows.ts:317 — re-verified: line 317 reads `await pages.commodityBasicDescription.btnSaveAndContinue.click();`).

fields.name: table.column.commodityCode
fields.label: Commodity code
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy: commodityID on CommodityComplement carries NO bean-validation annotation at all (/Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-imports-notification-schema/notification-schema-java/src/main/java/uk/gov/defra/tracesx/notificationschema/representation/CommodityComplement.java:21-22). The requirement to HAVE a commodity line is not a per-column @NotNull: the commodityComplement LIST's only @NotNull is under NotificationLowRiskFieldValidation, NOT NotificationChedppFieldValidation (Commodities.java:149-155). So at the schema level this read-back column is not individually mandatory for CHED-PP; required:false is legacy-correct. (As-old-system mandatoriness; the rebuild may model 'at least one commodity line' differently.)
fields.evidence: DISPLAY-ONLY read-back cell, not an input — this page renders no <select> and offers no options (re-verified: the adversarial pass's control inventory over <main> returns only crumb, etag, commodityDetailsPage and save-button). Rendered value '08059000' confirmed in trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572, action 28. The value originates on the Search Commodity page (page-objects/notification/SearchCommodityPage.ts — inputEnterCommodityCode), filled from config firstCommodityCode (workflows/notification/ched-pp-workflows.ts:300). SCHEMA CORRECTION BY THE ADVERSARIAL PASS: this list was previously carried under an `options` key with `optionsConfidence: inferred`, while the evidence text simultaneously admitted it 'is NOT an option list offered by this page'. An `options` key that is not options is inadmissible as an option list and invites exactly the fabricated-option-list failure it was hedging against, so it is renamed `testVocabulary`. It is the commodity-code vocabulary the TESTS use (types/commodity-code.ts:1-21 — 19 codes), not a rendered enumeration anywhere, and it is not known to be the app's full vocabulary. Only 08059000 (hmiOnlyCIDAC, types/commodity-code.ts:10) was ever observed here. CHED-PP defaults are 06011010 (hyacinths) and 06042090 (foliageOther) — ched-pp-workflows.ts:143-144, re-verified — neither of which was ever traced onto this page.

fields.name: table.column.genusAndSpecies
fields.label: Genus (and Species)
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy: complementName (the genus/species text this cell renders, template variations.complementName -> commoditySummary.html:53) carries NO bean-validation annotation on CommodityComplement (CommodityComplement.java:27-28); nor does eppoCode/speciesID/speciesName. No CHED-PP @NotNull applies to the species columns at the schema level, so required:false is legacy-correct (as-old-system).
fields.evidence: DISPLAY-ONLY read-back cell. 'Citrus australasica' confirmed in trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572, action 28 — re-verified character-for-character. Resolved by the service from the EPPO code searched on the Commodity Basic Description page (page-objects/notification/CommodityBasicDescriptionPage.ts:22-32 — inputEppoCode / btnSearch / btnAddSpecies), driven at workflows/notification/ched-pp-workflows.ts:308-310 (re-verified). The tests never assert the resolved species name on this page, so the lookup rule (EPPO code -> genus/species text) is the service's, not the test's.

fields.name: table.column.eppoCode
fields.label: EPPO code
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy: eppoCode on CommodityComplement carries NO bean-validation annotation (CommodityComplement.java:29) — no CHED-PP @NotNull. required:false is legacy-correct (as-old-system).
fields.evidence: DISPLAY-ONLY read-back cell. 'CIDAC' confirmed in trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572, action 28, and matches the only value in the domain type (types/eppo-code.ts:1-3, eppoCode.cidac) which ched-pp-hmi-checks.spec.ts:23 passes as firstEppoCode. SCHEMA CORRECTION BY THE ADVERSARIAL PASS: renamed from `options`/`optionsConfidence` to `testVocabulary` for the same reason as commodityCode above — this page offers no options, and the previous key asserted otherwise while the prose denied it. The list is a union of the domain type plus EPPO codes the CHED-PP specs pass as bare string literals: PHAAN (ched-pp-eu-harmonisation.spec.ts:25, ched-pp-notification.spec.ts:25), LENCU (ched-pp-eu-harmonisation.spec.ts:27, as secondEppoCode), NNNXX (ched-pp-eu-harmonisation.spec.ts:57, ched-pp-notification.spec.ts:91), CXQDA (ched-pp-notification.spec.ts:94, as secondEppoCode), GYPEL (ched-pp-date-validation.spec.ts:16 and passim). None of these except CIDAC was ever observed rendering on THIS page. NOTE: five of the six bypass the types/eppo-code.ts vocabulary — a QA-repo consistency smell, not a service requirement.

fields.name: table.column.variety
fields.label: Variety
fields.valueDomainScope: For species CIDAC only. Confirmed from the UPSTREAM select that produces this cell, not from this page — this page renders no select.
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy: variety is persisted as a ComplementParameterSet keyDataPair under the constant VARIETY="variety" (ComplementParameterSet.java:47). It carries NO @NotNull / NotNullKeyDataPair in ANY validation group — the only NotNullKeyDataPair on that class constrains TYPE_PACKAGE, and only under the CED/CVEDP groups (ComplementParameterSet.java:116-131). So variety is OPTIONAL in the CHED-PP model. This is 'as the old system had it' and the rebuild may revisit — but it corroborates the observed degenerate 'None'-only list and the fact that no test ever sets a variety.
fields.evidence: DISPLAY-ONLY read-back cell. Rendered literal 'None' confirmed in trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572, action 28. PROVENANCE INDEPENDENTLY RE-VERIFIED BY THE ADVERSARIAL PASS — 'None' is a REAL, USER-SELECTABLE OPTION, and for this species the ONLY one: the SAME trace, action 26 (the preceding page, H1 'Variety and class of commodity' verified by eval) renders <select name="add-variety-CIDAC"> whose complete option list is exactly ['None']. Because the select has a single option it is auto-selected with no user interaction, which is why 'None' reaches this table even though no test ever sets a variety (there is no variety field in ChedPPNotificationConfig, ched-pp-workflows.ts:44-160). So 'None' is neither a placeholder for unset nor a service default-for-blank — it is the selected value of a one-option reference-data list. SCHEMA NOTE: renamed `options` -> `valueDomain`, since these are the values this read-back cell can display (sourced upstream), not options this page offers. Corroborated by sibling spec pages/variety-of-genus-and-species.json.

fields.name: table.column.class
fields.label: Class
fields.valueDomainScope: For species CIDAC only — the template drives class options per species, so another species could in principle offer a different list. Confirmed from the UPSTREAM select, not from this page.
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy: class is persisted as a ComplementParameterSet keyDataPair under the constant CLASS="class" (ComplementParameterSet.java:48) with NO @NotNull / NotNullKeyDataPair in ANY validation group (ComplementParameterSet.java:116-131 constrains only TYPE_PACKAGE). Class is therefore OPTIONAL in the CHED-PP model. This SETTLES the blank-Class open question FROM THE MODEL SIDE: an unset/blank class is a legitimate persisted state (DEFAULT_CONFIG leaves commodityClass '' at ched-pp-workflows.ts:147), independent of whether any traced journey happens to render it. The remaining unknown is only what the frontend renders IN that blank cell (blank vs the 'Select class' placeholder), not whether the state is valid. Mandatoriness here is legacy policy the rebuild may revisit.
fields.conditionalOn: Populated only when the preceding 'Variety and class of commodity' page rendered and a class was chosen. Note on reading the QA workflow: lines 313-316 do not CAUSE the variety page to render — they are the test merely choosing to interact with a page the SERVICE decided to show. Trace cdb4ad0c proves the distinction: it takes the same EPPO branch with commodityClass unset and the variety page never appears at all, so there was nothing for lines 313-316 to skip. `commodityClass` is best read as a proxy the workflow author used for 'this commodity code shows a variety page', not as its trigger — see openQuestions for the experiment that separates the two. DEFAULT_CONFIG sets commodityClass to '' (ched-pp-workflows.ts:147, re-verified) and only ONE CHED-PP spec sets it — ched-pp-hmi-checks.spec.ts:24 (commodityClass.extraClass) — which is exactly the spec that produced our single trace. What the cell renders when no class was chosen is UNVERIFIED and may be UNREACHABLE on present evidence (see openQuestions).
fields.evidence: DISPLAY-ONLY read-back cell. 'Extra Class' confirmed in trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572, action 28. The three-value domain is CONFIRMED and was INDEPENDENTLY RE-VERIFIED by the adversarial pass: the SAME trace, action 26, renders <select name="add-class-CIDAC"> whose complete option list is ['Select class', 'Class I', 'Class II', 'Extra Class'] — 'Select class' being the placeholder top option and the three real values matching the domain vocabulary types/commodity-class.ts:1-5 (classI: 'Class I', classII: 'Class II', extraClass: 'Extra Class') exactly. The value is selected by label at ched-pp-workflows.ts:314 via page-objects/notification/CommodityClassPage.ts:6-8 (getByLabel('Select class')) — re-verified, and corroborated by action 26's own source pointer (ched-pp-workflows.ts:314) and action 27's (ched-pp-workflows.ts:315). Only 'Extra Class' was ever observed rendering in this table.

fields.name: table.column.removeAction
fields.label: (visually-hidden header: 'Remove action links')
fields.evidence: Action cell, EMPTY in the only trace we have — trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572, action 28: . The cell's emptiness in this single-row state is CONFIRMED (re-verified). From source: the cell holds a Remove control gated on {{#gt commodity.variations.length 1}} (commoditySummary.html:58), which is exactly why it rendered empty here — the trace had a single variation. It is NOT a link but a

validationMessages.message: You have added the maximum amount of commodity lines. You cannot add another variety and class.
validationMessages.trigger: Rendered on GET (never on submit) when the number of commodity lines already added is >= MAX_COMMODITY_LINES. It is an INFORMATIONAL NOTICE, not a validation error: it renders as a plain govuk-body-l paragraph, NOT in an error summary, and it simultaneously suppresses BOTH the 'Add another Genus (and Species)' link and the 'Add another commodity' link. 'Save and continue' remains available.
validationMessages.evidence: /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/commoditySummary.html:27-31 (the paragraph — copy re-verified VERBATIM by the adversarial pass, character for character; it is not invented), :67 ({{#unless @root.maxCommodityLinesReached}} around the 'Add another Genus' paragraph) and :77 ({{#unless maxCommodityLinesReached}} around 'Add another commodity') — both suppressions re-verified. Gate at commodity_summary.js:78 — maxCommodityLinesReached: numberOfCommodityLinesAdded >= MAX_COMMODITY_LINES (re-verified). Never observed in any trace and never exercised by any Playwright/E2E test. CORRECTION BY THE ADVERSARIAL PASS: the previous revision claimed this state was 'never exercised by any test' FLATLY. That is false and was an over-claim — the IPAFFS app's OWN unit tests exercise both branches of the flag: test/handlers/importer/commodity_summary_test.js:156-164 (mockNumberOfCommodityLines.returns(2) -> expects false) and :166-175 (returns(500) -> expects true). The claim is therefore correctly scoped to the Playwright/E2E corpus only. This matters: the flag's behaviour is not unverified, it is verified at the unit level — only its RENDERED appearance is untraced.

openQuestions: PAGE CONFIDENCE remains `inferred`, upheld by the adversarial pass. The confirmed observations all stand — every one was independently re-derived from the trace and none was contradicted. The page carries source-3 (IPAFFS app source) evidence for a page state and three form controls that no trace and no test ever touches, all of which are necessarily 'inferred'. Per the rule 'page confidence = the WEAKEST field confidence', the presence of inferred fields makes the page inferred.

ADVERSARIAL PASS VERDICT (recorded for the reader's calibration): the spec was checked against the raw trace independently and found substantially SOUND on its core claims. Specifically re-verified and UPHELD: the page identity (H1 'Commodity', title 'Commodity - Import and export applications - GOV.UK'); every rendered string character-for-character (caption, H1, H2, table headers, row values, both link texts, the button label); the complete control inventory over <main> (exactly crumb, etag, commodityDetailsPage, save-button — no missed fields); both upstream option lists at action 26 (add-variety-CIDAC = ['None'], add-class-CIDAC = ['Select class','Class I','Class II','Extra Class']); the govuk-!margin-bottom-9 typo sitting beside the correct spelling; the back link markup; every template and handler line number cited; the QA-repo zero-hit grep; the 1-of-59 journey count; and the cdb4ad0c counter-example (page-4 -> page-5, no variety page, no summary page). NO invented copy was found — including the max-lines notice, which is verbatim from source. The corrections below are real but secondary.

THE MAX_COMMODITY_LINES GAP IS NARROWER THAN THE PREVIOUS REVISION CLAIMED — CORRECTED BY THE ADVERSARIAL PASS. The previous text said the value 'is NOT determinable from any source available here'. That overstated it. The mechanism is fully determined (utils/constants.js:12 -> config.maxCommodityLines, re-verified) and the config files are readable: config/default.json:175 and config/k8s.json:169 both inject the placeholder "$$MAX_COMMODITY_LINES" from the environment, so the PRODUCTION value is indeed env-specific and still needs confirming with the IPAFFS team. But config/test.json:139 pins maxCommodityLines to 500, and the app's own unit test uses 500 as the boundary that flips the flag true (commodity_summary_test.js:172). So 500 is the repo's own working figure and a sound indicative starting point for the rebuild — the open question is only whether production overrides it, not what the cap means or roughly is.

NEW FINDING — THE CAP IS ALREADY EXPLAINED TO THE USER UPSTREAM, AND THE PREVIOUS REVISION MISSED IT. The Search Commodity / consignment page-3 template tells the user the limit before they hit it, with the number interpolated: 'You can only add {{maxCommodityLines}} commodity lines (commodity code and genus/species) to a notification. If you want to add more commodity lines, you can raise another notification.' (consignmentSelect.html:32, verbatim). This materially changes the content-design picture below: the cap is not sprung on the user without explanation, and the upstream copy is the better-written of the two. The rebuild should decide deliberately where to explain the cap — the IPAFFS answer is 'both upstream and at the point of exhaustion'.

CONTENT-DESIGN FLAG FOR A HUMAN (sharpened): this page's max-lines notice says 'You cannot add another variety and class' but the same flag ALSO suppresses 'Add another commodity' (commoditySummary.html:77). The copy under-describes what is actually blocked — a user reading it would reasonably expect to still be able to add a new commodity code, and would find that link gone. Note the upstream copy at consignmentSelect.html:32 gets this right ('You can only add N commodity lines... you can raise another notification'), which makes the summary page's wording an inconsistency within IPAFFS itself, not just a GDS nitpick. Worth fixing rather than porting.

NEW STATE FOUND, NEVER TRACED: the maximum-commodity-lines state. When lines added >= MAX_COMMODITY_LINES the page renders a notice and REMOVES both 'Add another...' affordances (see validationMessages). No trace and no Playwright test ever reaches it, though the app's own unit tests cover the flag. The rebuild needs an equivalent cap-and-explain behaviour.

THE NAMED-SUBMIT QUESTION IS NOW CLOSED — RESOLVED FROM SOURCE BY THE ADVERSARIAL PASS. The previous revision left an open question wondering whether name=save-button 'hints at other submit controls (e.g. a Save and exit) in states we never saw. Unverified.' It is now verified and the answer is NO. The template's button block (commoditySummary.html:81-85) renders exactly ONE save control unconditionally — no {{#if}} around it, no save-and-return or save-and-review variant in any state. The only other submit controls on the page are the per-row Remove buttons. Worth noting for the reader: the shared actionsModel DOES carry saveAndContinueButton/saveAndReturnButton/saveAndReviewButton ids (visible in the expected view context at commodity_summary_test.js:~118-121), which is presumably what prompted the original suspicion — but THIS template never renders the latter two. The model is shared across pages; this page uses one third of it. Do not design the rebuild around a save-and-exit that does not exist here.

THE SERVER MATCHES ON BUTTON LABELS — a fragile idiom worth carrying forward as a warning, not a pattern. The handler distinguishes submits by scanning the payload for a value of 'Remove' vs 'Save and continue' (commodity_summary.js:24,26 — both re-verified verbatim). The server therefore matches on the button's VISIBLE LABEL as its value, so changing the button copy would silently break the routing. Do not reproduce it.

SAVE AND CONTINUE ROUTING IS THREE-WAY AND ONLY ONE BRANCH WAS EVER TRACED (commodity_summary.js:47-53, re-verified). If partOne.purpose.purposeGroup is UNSET -> consignment/page-5 (the observed path, :48). If SET -> CHED-PP goes to the commodity-details BULK page (:51), all other CHED types go to the standard commodity-details page (:53). The rebuild should not assume the linear next-page seen in the trace.

THE 'NO VALIDATION' INFERENCE IS CONFIRMED FROM SOURCE. The POST handler (commodity_summary.js:21-58) contains no validation, no rejection path and no error rendering whatsoever — it branches only on which submit control was used, then redirects. The page genuinely cannot produce a validation error, which is unsurprising given it captures no user input. The single validationMessages entry is an informational notice rendered on GET, and is recorded there only because there is nowhere better for it.

LEGACY-SOURCE CONFIRMED ABSENCE OF VALIDATION COPY (legacy). The authoritative microservice ValidationMessages.properties was grepped for this page's columns — `grep -in 'variety|\.class\b|summary|commoditysummary'` over /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-notification-microservice/service/src/main/resources/ValidationMessages.properties returns ZERO hits, and no key named for the commodity-summary page exists. Every CHED-PP commodity-related message in that file (totalgrossweight/totalnetweight at :84-85, packages/quantity/net-weight/package-type at :130-134, 'A commodity must be selected' at :91 — the last being euimp/LowRisk, not chedpp) belongs to the UPSTREAM commodity-details BULK page (partone.commodities.*), NOT to this summary. This is the legacy-side confirmation of the trace finding that only ~8 of ~155 messages ever rendered: none of the unseen CHED-PP messages apply to THIS page. There is nothing to add to validationMessages beyond the one informational max-lines notice already recorded.

LEGACY MODEL MANDATORINESS (legacy) — this page's read-back columns are all OPTIONAL / UNCONSTRAINED at the CHED-PP schema level, so required:false on every field is legacy-correct, not merely a trace inference. Details: commodityID / complementName / eppoCode carry no bean-validation annotation on CommodityComplement (CommodityComplement.java:21-29); variety and class are ComplementParameterSet keyDataPair values (constants VARIETY="variety" ComplementParameterSet.java:47, CLASS="class" :48) with no @NotNull in any group (the only NotNullKeyDataPair on that class is TYPE_PACKAGE under CED/CVEDP, :116-131); and the commodityComplement LIST itself is @NotNull only under NotificationLowRiskFieldValidation, NOT NotificationChedppFieldValidation (Commodities.java:149-155). The CHED-PP @NotNull group on Commodities instead binds coarser aggregate fields — totalGrossWeight (:72-80) and totalNetWeight (:91-99) — which are captured on the commodity-details bulk page, not here. CAVEAT: this is 'mandatoriness as the old system modelled it'. The rebuild may legitimately choose to make species/class mandatory, or to enforce 'at least one commodity line' as a first-class rule rather than leaning on the LowRisk-group @NotNull; that is a policy decision, not a defect to port.

THIS PAGE CAPTURES NO USER INPUT. Every control in <main> is either hidden state (crumb/etag/commodityDetailsPage) or the submit button — INDEPENDENTLY re-verified by the adversarial pass with its own control inventory eval over <main>, which returns exactly those four and nothing else. It is a read-back/confirmation step over data gathered on the preceding Search Commodities (page-3), Select species (page-4) and 'Variety and class of commodity' pages. The rebuild should treat it as a display page, and should ask whether it is needed at all given the journey already ends in a full 'Review Notification' page. CORROBORATED by source 2: the QA repo has no page object for this page and no test asserts anything about it — it is purely passed through.

THE PAGE IS NOT REACHED MERELY BY TAKING THE EPPO BRANCH — a trace-backed correction that still stands. COUNTER-EVIDENCE re-verified by the adversarial pass: trace cdb4ad0ce1d43b01f6b28ae8b2a32c1a1861f2f5 (ched-pp-date-validation.spec.ts:9, 'CHED-PP Arrival to BCP Date in the past') sets firstEppoCode: 'GYPEL' — i.e. it TAKES the EPPO branch (ched-pp-workflows.ts:304) — and yet its journey goes straight from consignment/page-4 to consignment/page-5 (independently re-confirmed by grepping the journey extract: it contains page-1, page-2 x2, page-3, page-4, page-5 and NEITHER variety-of-genus-and-species NOR commodity/summary). So taking the EPPO branch is NOT sufficient to reach this page. This matters because ched-pp-date-validation.spec.ts sets firstEppoCode in TEN separate invocations; had the EPPO-branch theory held, this page would be among the best-covered in the corpus rather than the worst.

WHAT ACTUALLY DISCRIMINATES — TWO CONFOUNDED CANDIDATES, AND THIS IS THE PAGE'S REAL OPEN QUESTION FOR A HUMAN. The variety page and this summary page CO-OCCUR EXACTLY: of 59 CHED-PP journey extracts, exactly ONE contains `commodity/summary` — the journey e10b7cd5 (count independently re-verified by the adversarial pass: `grep -rl` returns 1, and the extract directory holds 59 files). Comparing the only two journeys that both take the EPPO branch isolates the difference to the commodity/species pair, not the branch: (a) e10b7cd5 — code 08059000, EPPO CIDAC, commodityClass SET -> page-4 -> variety-of-genus-and-species?commodity-selected-code=08059000 -> commodity/summary -> page-5. (b) cdb4ad0c — code 0603197090 (commodityCode.cutFlowers), EPPO GYPEL, commodityClass UNSET -> page-4 -> page-5, nothing in between. TWO CANDIDATE EXPLANATIONS REMAIN AND THE CORPUS CANNOT SEPARATE THEM, because they co-vary perfectly across the only two data points: (1) the commodity code / species carries variety+class reference data (08059000/CIDAC does, 0603197090/GYPEL does not), so the service inserts the variety page and its summary; or (2) selecting a class is what creates a commodity line worth summarising. Candidate (1) is the more plausible reading — the QA workflow's `if (commodityClass)` (line 313) can only ever have been written by an author who already knew 08059000/CIDAC shows a variety page, so `commodityClass` looks like a PROXY for the reference-data property rather than the cause. But that is reasoning about intent, not evidence. FOR A HUMAN / FOR ONE DELIBERATE TRACE: run commodity code 08059000 + CIDAC with commodityClass UNSET. If the variety and summary pages still render, candidate (1) is confirmed and the rebuild needs this page only for species that carry variety/class reference data. If they do not, candidate (2) holds. This single experiment decides whether the rebuild needs the page at all, or only in a variant — and it also settles the blank-Class question below.

THE PAGE IS INVISIBLE TO THE PLAYWRIGHT SUITE — re-verified as a complete negative. A grep for `ommoditySummary|commodity-summary|commodity/summary` across the ENTIRE QA repo (ipaffs-qa-automation) returns ZERO hits — re-run independently by the adversarial pass. There is no page object, no locator, no URL assertion, no navigation helper and no test that names this page anywhere in the automation suite. The workflow drives the submit through `pages.commodityBasicDescription.btnSaveAndContinue` (page-objects/notification/CommodityBasicDescriptionPage.ts:34-36) at ched-pp-workflows.ts:317 — i.e. a locator belonging to a DIFFERENT page, matching only by the generic role+name 'Save and continue' (line 317 re-verified verbatim: `await pages.commodityBasicDescription.btnSaveAndContinue.click();`, and action 28's own source pointer confirms it). The page is therefore clicked through, never asserted, and would not fail a test if it regressed or vanished. Recommend the rebuild ships a real page object (or drops the page). Caveat added: 'invisible to the suite' means the E2E suite — the app's own unit tests DO cover this handler (test/handlers/importer/commodity_summary_test.js), including the view name, the full view context, the actionsModel query-param plumbing and both max-lines branches. That is the only automated coverage this page has.

THIS PAGE IS NOT ACCESSIBILITY-TESTED. tests/accessibility/ched-pp-accessibility-tests.spec.ts names every CHED-PP page it checks — 'Search Commodity Page (first)', 'Commodity Basic Description Page (first)', 'Commodity Extended Description Page' x4, and others — and 'Commodity Summary' is not among them. Consistent with the a11y journey using the non-EPPO branch. Given the page has a 6-column side-scrolling table, a visually-hidden caption and a visually-hidden action-column header, that is exactly the surface an a11y check would earn its keep on.

THE H2 IS DATA, NOT COPY — do NOT hardcode 'Other'. The H2 is {{commodity.commodityDescription}} (commoditySummary.html:34, re-verified), sourced from uniqueComplement.commodityDescription on the notification's commodityComplement collection (commodity_summary.js:89, re-verified). So 'Other' is simply how commodity code 08059000 is described; a different code renders different H2 text. The multi-commodity layout is likewise confirmed from source rather than inferred from the id scheme: {{#each commodities as |commodity|}} at commoditySummary.html:33 wraps H2 + table + 'Add another Genus (and Species)' as one repeating block, and the collection is built by de-duplicating complements on commodityID (commodity_summary.js:86 — _.uniqBy(complements, 'commodityID'), re-verified). The trace-coverage point stands: ched-pp-hmi-checks.spec.ts:20 sets singleCommodity: true, so the repeated shape has never been RENDERED by any test.

THE REMOVE CONTROL'S SUPPRESSION RULE IS TEMPLATE-CONFIRMED: it is suppressed when only one row remains, {{#gt commodity.variations.length 1}} at commoditySummary.html:58 (re-verified). Two points of precision: it is a submit BUTTON not a link (so it has no href), and the gate is per-COMMODITY-GROUP variations, so you cannot remove the last line of a given commodity code even when other commodity codes are present. Full detail in the remove_species_<uniqueComplementId> field. Still never rendered in a trace and never exercised by a Playwright test, so it stays inferred.

What does the Class column render when no class was chosen? STILL A GAP, and the dilemma is settled in one direction. Only ched-pp-hmi-checks.spec.ts:24 sets commodityClass, and DEFAULT_CONFIG leaves it '' (ched-pp-workflows.ts:147, re-verified). The class select's default is the 'Select class' placeholder (confirmed at action 26), so the cell may render blank, the placeholder text, or nothing at all. The page's rendering condition is narrower than the workflow's branching implies: ched-pp-notification.spec.ts:25 and ched-pp-eu-harmonisation.spec.ts:25 set firstEppoCode ('PHAAN') but leave firstCommodityCode at the DEFAULT hyacinths/06011010 (ched-pp-workflows.ts:143, re-verified), and no journey of theirs reaches this page at all — exactly as the directly-observed GYPEL/cutFlowers counter-example (cdb4ad0c) shows for the same shape. So the blank-Class state is not merely unobserved: on present evidence there may be NO reachable route to it, because every journey that omits a class also fails to render the page. The blank-Class cell and the page's own render condition are therefore the SAME question, and the one experiment proposed above (08059000 + CIDAC + no class) settles both at once. Do not assume a blank Class cell exists in the rebuild until that trace is run.

AN UNTRACED SPECIES-SELECTION VARIANT EXISTS: `isSpeciesACheckbox`. The workflow offers a SECOND way to choose a species inside the EPPO branch — `pages.commodityBasicDescription.checkboxChedPPSpecies(firstEppoCode).check()` (ched-pp-workflows.ts:305-306, re-verified), locating `#${species}-checkbox` (page-objects/notification/CommodityBasicDescriptionPage.ts:14-16) instead of the EPPO textbox + Search + 'Add species' sequence (lines 308-310, re-verified). It is exercised by ched-pp-eu-harmonisation.spec.ts:55 (isSpeciesACheckbox: true, firstEppoCode: 'NNNXX') and ched-pp-notification.spec.ts:90 (same pair). Both leave firstCommodityCode at the default, so neither reaches this page and the variant is untraced HERE — recorded because it means the upstream species-selection page has two distinct UI shapes (a checkbox list vs an EPPO search), and whichever shape is used, the resulting row still has to render in this table. The rebuild's species-selection design should account for both.

VARIETY/CLASS ARE ONE PAGE, NOT TWO. The page preceding this one is a single page at /commodity/variety-of-genus-and-species with H1 'Variety and class of commodity' (re-verified by eval at action 26; its <title> is 'Variety of Genus (and Species) - Import and export applications - GOV.UK', which does not match its own H1 — noted for the sibling spec) carrying BOTH the variety select (add-variety-CIDAC) and the class select (add-class-CIDAC). The QA repo's page object for it is misleadingly named `CommodityClassPage` and exposes ONLY the class dropdown — it has no locator for the variety dropdown. So the accurate statement is not 'there is no page object for the Variety page' but 'the variety dropdown is untargeted by any page object, and no test ever sets a variety'. See sibling spec pages/variety-of-genus-and-species.json.

VARIETY OPTIONS ARE PER-SPECIES REFERENCE DATA, AND WE HAVE ONLY SEEN A DEGENERATE LIST. For CIDAC the variety select offers exactly one option, 'None' (confirmed, action 26) — so we have never seen what a populated variety list looks like, where 'None' sits within one, or whether 'None' is present for every species. The rebuild should source variety options from a reference-data service rather than hard-code 'None'. SEPARATE GDS QUESTION FOR A HUMAN: even though 'None' is a legitimate selected value, rendering the word 'None' in a table cell is not GOV.UK house style — the Design System would normally leave the cell blank or say 'Not provided'. Worth a content decision in the new app.

LEGACY-ENRICHMENT PASS (legacy) — re-verified the three authoritative IPAFFS sources directly and found NOTHING to add or firm up beyond what the adversarial pass already recorded. (1) TEMPLATE: /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/commoditySummary.html read in full — every control, conditional branch ({{#if actionsModel.fromImporterReview}}/{{else if fromPartTwoOverview}} at :21-25, {{#if maxCommodityLinesReached}} notice at :27-31, the per-row {{#gt commodity.variations.length 1}} Remove button at :58-59, both {{#unless ...maxCommodityLinesReached}} link suppressions at :67 and :77), and every copy string already appears in this spec verbatim. No missed field, no conditional {{#if isChedpp}} branch, no additional input exists in this template. (2) VALIDATION: `grep -in 'chedpp|commoditysummary|variety|summary'` over ValidationMessages.properties re-confirms ZERO keys for this page — the CHED-PP commodity messages at :84-85 (totalgrossweight/totalnetweight) and :130-134 (number_package/type_package/net_weight/type_quantity/quantity keydatapairs) all belong to the upstream commodity-details bulk page, not this summary. There is genuinely nothing to add to validationMessages beyond the one informational max-lines notice. (3) MANDATORINESS: CommodityComplement.java re-read — commodityID (:22), complementName (:28), eppoCode (:29) carry NO annotation of any kind; Commodities.java re-read — the CHED-PP @NotNull group binds only totalGrossWeight and totalNetWeight, and the commodityComplement LIST is @NotNull only under NotificationLowRiskFieldValidation, NOT NotificationChedppFieldValidation. So required:false on every field on this page is legacy-correct, as already stated. CAVEAT UNCHANGED: this is 'mandatoriness as the old system modelled it' — the rebuild may legitimately choose to make species/class mandatory or enforce 'at least one commodity line' as a first-class CHED-PP rule. No discrepancy between legacy copy and the confirmed rendered values was found; the two agree.

ONLY ONE TRACE (e10b7cd5) IS KNOWN TO REACH THIS PAGE, and only via the species/variety path. That trace is POSITIVELY IDENTIFIED as tests/notification/ched-pp/ched-pp-hmi-checks.spec.ts:15 — '[IMTA-20591] CHED-PP HMI auto-completion > should auto-complete the HMI check outcome when risk decision is no inspection' (trace Title independently re-verified by the adversarial pass via `trace open`; the trace reports 127 actions, 5 pages, 0 errors). Its config (spec lines 18-26) matches the rendered row exactly: singleCommodity: true, firstCommodityCode: commodityCode.hmiOnlyCIDAC = '08059000', firstEppoCode: eppoCode.cidac = 'CIDAC', commodityClass: commodityClass.extraClass = 'Extra Class'. Every rendered cell is accounted for: 'Citrus australasica' by the service-side EPPO lookup, and 'None' by the single-option variety select at action 26. Scope caveat on the denominator: 1 of the 59 journey extracts under workareas/trace-requirements/ched-pp/journeys/ (count re-verified), not 1 of the 65 CHED-PP traces the brief cites, so 6 CHED-PP traces have no extract and were not searched. The raw traces are zipped and cannot be grepped directly, so closing that last 6 would need the trace CLI. The substantive point stands: coverage is single-row, single-commodity, happy-path, no removals, and incidental to an HMI test. Treat the coverage, not the page, as the risk.
